title: Philosophy of Language
description: Reference, meaning, speech acts — Frege, Wittgenstein, Austin in one accessible hour.
summary_pt_br: Filosofia da linguagem: sentido vs referência, jogos de linguagem, atos de fala. A linguagem age no mundo.

scenes.subtitle_en: Reference, meaning, speech acts — Frege, Wittgenstein, Austin in one accessible hour.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Filosofia da linguagem: sentido vs referência, jogos de linguagem, atos de fala. A linguagem age no mundo.

scenes.text_en: Reference, meaning, speech acts — Frege, Wittgenstein, Austin in one accessible hour. Focus: philosophy of language. Sense is how we refer; reference is what we refer to.
scenes.text_pt: Filosofia da linguagem: sentido vs referência, jogos de linguagem, atos de fala. A linguagem age no mundo. Foco: philosophy of language. Sentido é como referimos; referência é o que referimos.

scenes.title: Philosophy of Language
scenes.body_en: Key ideas: SENSE vs REFERENCE (Frege). LANGUAGE GAMES (Wittgenstein). SPEECH ACTS — locutionary/illocutionary/perlocutionary (Austin). Language acts on the world, not just describes it.
scenes.body_pt: Filosofia da linguagem: sentido vs referência, jogos de linguagem, atos de fala. A linguagem age no mundo.

examples.en: Sense is how we refer; reference is what we refer to.
examples.pt: Sentido é como referimos; referência é o que referimos.

examples.en: Meaning arises from use, not definition.
examples.pt: Significado emerge do uso, não da definição.

examples.en: 'I promise' is not describing — it's performing.

exercise.question: Complete: "Sense is how we refer; ___ is what we refer to."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "Sense is how we refer; reference is what we refer to."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Sentido é como referimos; referência é o que referimos.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
Meaning arises from use, not definition.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "Meaning arises from use, not definition."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Significado emerge do uso, não da definição.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Philosophy of Language — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre philosophy of language. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Morning star and evening star refer to Venus.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Meaning is how words get used in life.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: 'I promise' is an act, not a description.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: What the speaker does by saying it.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Language is action dressed as description.

scenes.title: The philosophy seminar
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize three philosophers' key ideas. Use at least three philosophical terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In the small philosophy seminar, Kumar guided students through a century of debates about meaning.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Frege separated sense from reference — morning star and evening star both refer to Venus but mean differently.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Wittgenstein argued that meaning emerges from use — language games we learn by living, not by defining.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Austin showed us that saying 'I promise' is not describing a promise — it is performing one through the words themselves.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: So illocutionary force is what we do with an utterance, beyond what it literally says about the world.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Philosophy of language, Kumar believed, teaches you to feel the weight every sentence carries into the next moment.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Philosophy of Language". Reference, meaning, speech acts — Frege, Wittgenstein, Austin in one accessible hour. Sense is how we refer; reference is what we refer to. Meaning arises from use, not definition. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Philosophy of Language". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
check.explanation: She says the title: "Philosophy of Language".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Philosophy of Language".

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
